issues.reviewReason: Big one — shipping it in safe, verifiable increments rather than one risky rewrite. KEY FINDING from a deep multi-agent investigation: most of this flow is ALREADY built, so I'm editing in place, not rebuilding (full plan saved in the repo at docs/PLAN_BUILD_REDESIGN_i-20260620-1469.md). DONE NOW (increment 1, verified on the dev server): the 'How should we build your plan?' 2-path screen is GONE — after Health & Food you go straight into Build Your Workout. REMAINING increments need two quick decisions from you: (A) BLUR/FOCUS on the builder screens — a previous design review deliberately removed blur as 'no glassmorphism'. Your new ask reverses that. OK to bring a calm focus-dim back (dim/blur everything except 'Let coach build it all' + a quiet 'I'll build myself')? (B) The 'coach is building' DOUBLE-CHECK that re-checks workout/diet/activity vs your goal needs a backend change to the plan-finalize step + a deploy; I'd ship it observe-only first (it never blocks finishing onboarding). Also queued, no decision needed: fix the sedentary-user step target (showing ~11.5k for a sedentary profile), and consolidate the 'your plan is ready' page to the coach-profile style with a single scroll-revealed 'Continue to dashboard'. Reply with a yes/no on A and B and I'll proceed; otherwise I'll do the no-decision ones (steps + plan-ready) next.

history.note: also host the articles on the websites and create new articles for seo and do all the seo things on the website, for each exercise write one article and post it use the exercise image we have and then post it the same image also do that for all the meals with the images recipe write fitness articles and incorporated nyus reference and urge users to download and how it helps make tracking and achive the fitness goal easily with nyus app

previousFix.description: Expanded the Help & FAQ to comprehensively cover every feature. It now has 15 categories and 46 articles: Getting Around, Nutrition & Diet, Tracking, Workouts, Progress & Insights, Social & Partners, Achievements, Personal Coach (AI), Plan Management, Wellness, Notifications, Subscription, Account, Data & Privacy, and Troubleshooting — covering onboarding, dashboard, meal logging/swaps/macros, weight & measurements, workouts/progressive-overload/PRs, Health Score, weekly review, body composition, friends/leaderboards/partner groups, XP/levels/badges, goal change/pause/weekly updates, wellness check-ins, notifications, Health Connect sync, offline, and more. Screenshot shows the expanded list live on the FAQ screen. Note: the backend also has two leftover 'test'/'test2' help categories — those are backend data you can remove from the admin content panel whenever; the app content itself is now comprehensive.

issues.description: For thai question show nice progress bars animation nice ui elements planned deeply also for similar other use the same ui

fix.description: Here's the response UI you asked to see (screenshot). When you tap 'Discuss all metrics with Coach', the body-composition message now renders in chat as a metric grid: progress bars for body fat (20.9%), muscle rate (37.6%), body water (57.6%), and clean stat tiles for Weight, BMI, visceral fat, BMR, TDEE, metabolic age (30 yrs), fitness age, VO2 max, FFMI, goal, and your daily protein/water target — with your question as the header. Live in v124 on alpha (also reuses for similar structured messages).

previousFix.description: Done. When you tap 'Discuss all metrics with Coach', your body-composition message now renders as a clean metric grid instead of a plain bullet wall: a 2-column set of tiles with progress bars for the percentage metrics (body fat, muscle rate, body water) and tidy stat tiles for Weight, BMI, visceral fat, BMR, TDEE, metabolic age, fitness age, VO2 max, FFMI, goal and your daily protein/water target — with your question as the header. The coach still receives the full numbers, so its advice is unchanged. Screenshot shows it live in chat. Built as a reusable pattern, so similar 'review these numbers' messages can use the same rich UI next. (Note: this is the same body-comp message where metabolic age now reads a sensible '30 yrs' per the earlier fix.)
